need to be a client of 42 macro so let's
just hop right in so number one uh the
December University of Michigan consumer
sentiment survey supported consensus
expectations of a soft Landing in the US
economy number two uh we continue to
believe that predicting whether the
economy soft hard or no lands is a
Fool's errant asset markets reserve the
right to price in multiple economic
scenarios in succession it is our job as
investors to make and save money along
the way regardless of outcome hence our
choice to position according to our
medium-term quantitative riskmanagement
signals rather than exposing ourselves
to the confirmation bias that comes from
Pigeon ho holding ourselves into one of
those three camps right now our
medium-term Quant signals are unequip
equivocally bullish will communicate and
reposition when that changes and then
lastly number three in China the
November and November CPI and PBI data
supported expectations of incremental
policy easing we continue to believe
Beijing is gearing up the frontload
policy support for 2024 early in the New
Year refer to our December 5th lead off
morning note for more details regarding
that outcome transitioning to my 42
macro dashboard our beautiful 42 macro
dashboard this morning we'll start with
our signals from our friends over at B
in terms of trading ranges the vix is
the only thing overbought oversold uh
this morning you're getting close to the
lower boundary that vix range here uh
important that's that's heading into a
pretty big week for economics uh uh
economic and policy updates you got CPI
from the US on Tuesday PPI from the US
uh uh on Wednesday uh retail sales on
Thursday here in the US and then
industrial production on Friday in the
US you got Chinese hard data retail
sales industri production F asset
investment and the unemployment rate uh
on Friday as well and then then
Wednesday we have the FMC at 2m and then
on Thursday we got the bank of England
and ECB policy meetings as well so vixs
at the low end of the range heading into
a a big week suggest that you're
probably not going to see the kind of
you know Vana flows that you tend to see
on the other side of you know the CPI
prints or the uh fomc prints you know
the markets are pretty uh pretty coales
around a soft Landing consensus right
now so we do need to see both data and
policy updates continue to support that
consensus uh we expect they will just
given the evolution of the data um over
the last few weeks in terms of inflation
and and the labor market uh and then
transition to the 42 macro Weare
Community we get a question here uh
regarding our qualitative research uh
summary uh so one of the aspects of our
qualitative research summar is we help
investors understand which economies
abroad have the best growth and
inflation Dynamics uh and policy
Dynamics in so much that we also help
them understand which economies abroad
have the worst the least favorable uh
growth inflation and policy Dynamics and
so Canada uh is one of the economies
that has one of the more favorable
growth Dynamics at the current juncture
and how we're deriving that as we have
you know it's it's it's born out of our
our systematic uh long short signals
that are in our um in our in our in our
Global liquidity monitor table uh and so
right now what we're looking for on the
long side of an equity Market from a
growth standpoint uh is an acceleration
a trending acceleration in the leading
indicator for growth uh that's the
composite PMI uh for in that particular
table and then we also want to see
positive economic surprises on a
trending basis and right now Canada is
actually the only economy that has a
trending Rising composite PMI on a trend
basis and a trend of higher positive
economics surprises and so it's no
surprise that Canada has the most
favorable growth outlooks or growth
Dynamics um you know in so much that you
know we're on the negative side of
growth we're looking for a lower Trend
in the composite PMI and a trend of
economic uh uh disappointment uh in in
those particular economies uh so that's
uh that's one of those things so
Canada's growth on an absolute level was
not great
but again markets don't function on
absolutes you know the growth's good or
growth's bad markets function on going
from good to bad or from bad to bad good
and in everywhere in between it's about
the direction of travel and it's about
you know the direction of travel
relative to consensus expectations which
is why we're looking at direction of
travel and pol and and and economic
surprises as those two factors to
determine who which economies have the
most favorable Endor least favorable
growth Dynamics in that table similar
process we use for inflation on the
inflation side we're looking for the
headline and see in core CBI
decelerating with negative inflation
surprises on the on the on the that's on
the most favorable side for inflation on
the least favorable side for inflation
we're looking for economies that have
accelerating uh headline and core CPI uh
with positive inflation surprises and
then lastly on the policy front uh we're
looking for in that uh for for economies
that have favorable policy we're looking
for a trend higher in their specific
Count's liquidity proxy so recall that
we've created a a global liquidity proxy
which is aggregated sum of the global
central bank balance sheet Global broad
money supply and Global FX reserves
minus gold we'd run that same process
for every major economy that feeds into
that Global liquidity proxy and so on
the policy front we're looking for uh
Rising uh the country level liquidity
proxy and we're also looking for a
declining policy rat so that'd be a very
positive uh policy dynamic in an economy
in so much that a falling liquidity
proxy and a rising policy rate would be
uh the opposite of that which is a
negative so that's then for those of you
who are obviously reviewing our
qualitative research summary uh on a
daily and or weekly basis and our lead
up morning note and around the horn at
our monthly basis and our macros SC
report as well uh that that's how we get
